marketState: neutral-to-bearish - price sits at fair value while technicals and storage skew lower, offset by hot weather demand
summary: Front price ($2.924) is trading almost exactly at modeled fair value ($2.917 now, with spreads staying inside a few cents through +21D), so there is no clean valuation edge in either direction. Seasonality is directionally soft (probHigher 38/38/50/31% over 1-4wk, typical 4-week move -3.1%) but reliability is explicitly Low (50pp spread), and price is already running well below the seasonal median (-14.2% YTD vs -3.4% median). All six technical timeframes (5m through 1w) show bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross, and the daily candle has closed below its lower Bollinger band - a clean short-term downtrend signal - though intraday RSI (23-33) is already stretched toward oversold. Storage is the strongest structural bearish input: +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a +72 Bcf bearish surprise build. Weather is the main counterweight - both the 1-7D and 8-16D demand windows are running roughly 11% above 5-year norms in the upper quartile of range, supporting price and potentially shrinking the storage surplus in coming weeks. COT managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile of its 5-year range, i.e. not extreme, leaving room for either continuation or a short-covering bounce. Production data was not present in this snapshot.

longTerm.rationale: The multi-week picture is genuinely mixed: bearish storage (+184 Bcf vs 5y, +72 Bcf bearish surprise) and a weak-bearish, low-reliability seasonality tilt are offset by hot weather demand (+10.8%/+11.7% vs 5y) that could tighten the surplus, while price sits almost exactly at fair value with no band-edge discount or premium to lean on.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either a rally into the $3.05-$3.15 zone (fair-value resistance) to sell, or confirmation from the next 1-2 EIA storage reports on whether hot weather is meaningfully shrinking the surplus (bullish) versus builds staying large despite the heat (bearish continuation).

shortTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.95 (sell rallies toward front price / prior close $2.94)
shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim to 0.5% given intraday RSI already oversold
shortTerm.rationale: All six technical timeframes are aligned bearish (50<200 EMA cross) and the daily bar has broken below its lower Bollinger band, confirming active downside momentum; the mild front-Sep backwardation (-$0.046) reflects soft prompt-month sentiment rather than physical tightness, and price is fairly valued so there is no valuation headwind against the trade.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back above roughly $3.05, or a sharp RSI turn-up alongside hot weather pulling spot demand higher, would invalidate the short-term breakdown thesis.

keyDrivers: All 6 technical timeframes bearish trend with 50<200 EMA death cross; daily price closed below the lower Bollinger band
Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build
Weather demand running hot (+10.8%/+11.7% vs 5y, upper quartile of range) supporting near-term price
Price essentially at fair value across all horizons (spreads within a few cents of fair)
Seasonality tilt is bearish but unreliable (Low reliability, 50pp spread; probHigher 31-38% most weeks)
risks: COT managed money already net short (35th percentile) - risk of a short-covering squeeze if a bullish catalyst (heat wave, storage miss) hits
Intraday RSI (23-33) already oversold, raising the chance of a sharp relief bounce against short positions
Winter curve carries steep contango (Dec/Jan near $3.8-$4.2) reflecting weather-driven upside tail risk if heating demand surprises
